questions.question: Explain how the area of a rectangle can be determined using unit squares.
questions.answer: A rectangle can be divided into unit squares arranged in rows and columns. The number of squares equals length × width, giving the area.

questions.question: A rectangle has length 9 cm and width 5 cm. Explain how to calculate its area and show the steps.
questions.answer: Area = length × width = 9 × 5 = 45 cm².

questions.question: Two rectangles have dimensions 7×4 cm and 8×3 cm. Compare their areas and explain which is larger.
questions.answer: Area1 = 28 cm² and Area2 = 24 cm². The rectangle with sides 7 cm and 4 cm has greater area.

questions.question: Explain how drawing a diagonal divides a rectangle into triangles and describe the relationship between their areas.
questions.answer: The diagonal divides the rectangle into two congruent triangles, each having half the area of the rectangle.

questions.question: A triangle has base 10 cm and height 6 cm. Explain how to calculate its area.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × base × height = 1/2 × 10 × 6 = 30 cm².

questions.question: Explain why triangles having the same base and height have equal areas.
questions.answer: Area depends only on base and height. If both are equal, the areas will also be equal.

questions.question: The area of a triangle is 40 cm² and its base is 8 cm. Explain how to find its height.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × base × height. So 40 = 1/2 × 8 × h → h = 10 cm.

questions.question: Explain how the area of a parallelogram can be derived from a rectangle.
questions.answer: A triangular portion of the parallelogram can be cut and moved to the other side, forming a rectangle with the same base and height.

questions.question: A parallelogram has base 12 cm and height 7 cm. Explain how to find its area.
questions.answer: Area = base × height = 12 × 7 = 84 cm².

questions.question: Explain the importance of height in calculating the area of a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Height represents the perpendicular distance between the bases, which determines the area covered.

questions.question: A rhombus has diagonals 16 cm and 12 cm. Explain how to calculate its area.
Explain how the diagonals of a rhombus help derive its area formula.
questions.answer: The diagonals divide the rhombus into four triangles, which helps derive the formula using half the product of diagonals.

questions.question: Explain how the area formula of a trapezium is derived.
questions.answer: The trapezium can be divided into rectangles and triangles or combined with another trapezium to form a parallelogram.

questions.question: A trapezium has parallel sides 10 cm and 14 cm and height 6 cm. Calculate its area.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × height × (sum of parallel sides) = 1/2 × 6 × (10+14) = 72 cm².

questions.question: Explain why two identical trapeziums can form a parallelogram.
questions.answer: When placed together appropriately, their parallel sides align to create a parallelogram shape.

questions.question: Explain how the area of a pentagon can be calculated using triangles.
questions.answer: A pentagon can be divided into several triangles, and the sum of their areas gives the total area.

questions.question: Explain how a quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles to calculate its area.
questions.answer: Draw a diagonal across the quadrilateral to form two triangles and calculate their areas separately.

questions.question: Explain the importance of using diagrams while solving area problems.
questions.answer: Diagrams help identify shapes, base, and height clearly, making calculations easier.

questions.question: Explain how the area of a classroom floor can be calculated.
questions.answer: Measure the length and width of the classroom floor and multiply them to get the area.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between square units and area measurement.
questions.answer: Area is measured in square units because it represents a two-dimensional surface.

questions.question: Explain how the area of an irregular shape can be estimated.
questions.answer: Irregular shapes can be divided into known shapes such as rectangles and triangles and their areas added.

questions.question: Explain why area formulas are important in construction and engineering.
questions.answer: They help measure land, building floors, and construction materials accurately.

questions.question: Explain how the midpoint of a triangle side affects the areas of resulting triangles.
questions.answer: Joining a vertex to the midpoint divides the triangle into two equal areas.

questions.question: Explain how geometric dissection helps understand area formulas.
questions.answer: Dissection rearranges shapes into known figures like rectangles to derive formulas.

questions.question: Explain the importance of understanding area in everyday life.
questions.answer: Area calculations are useful for measuring land, designing buildings, and estimating material requirements.
